evals.prompt: I want to add MatrixScan Batch to my React Native app to simultaneously track multiple barcodes. Here's my screen file: EmptyApp.tsx. I need to scan EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes and show highlight frames around each one.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, writes complete BarcodeBatch* integration code into EmptyApp.tsx as a function component with hooks, renders a DataCaptureView, wires the camera lifecycle with BarcodeBatch.createRecommendedCameraSettings, calls dataCaptureContext.setMode, and shows the setup checklist (packages, npx pod-install, camera permission, license key placeholder).

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown (mentions scandit-react-native-datacapture-core and scandit-react-native-datacapture-barcode)
Setup checklist mentions npx pod-install for iOS
Setup checklist mentions NSCameraUsageDescription in ios Info.plist
DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present (v8 API)
DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is NOT present
DataCaptureContext.sharedInstance is present (semantic)
'-- ENTER YOUR SCANDIT LICENSE KEY HERE --' is present
new BarcodeBatch(settings) is present (constructor pattern, not forDataCaptureContext factory)
Symbology.EAN13UPCA is enabled
Symbology.Code128 is enabled
<DataCaptureView is rendered in the JSX tree
DataCaptureView has a context prop (semantic)
dataCaptureContext.setMode( is called with the BarcodeBatch instance
camera.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.On) is present
The component is written as a function component (no class syntax)
useRef is used to hold the BarcodeBatch mode reference
dataCaptureContext.removeMode( is called in the useEffect cleanup
Import from 'scandit-react-native-datacapture-core' is present
Import from 'scandit-react-native-datacapture-barcode' is present

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan Batch app I need to react to the barcodes being tracked. Show me how to receive addedTrackedBarcodes, updatedTrackedBarcodes, and removedTrackedBarcodes from each frame, and also how to iterate all currently tracked barcodes. File: EmptyApp.tsx.
evals.expected_output: The skill implements a BarcodeBatchListener with didUpdateSession, accesses session.addedTrackedBarcodes, session.updatedTrackedBarcodes, session.removedTrackedBarcodes, and iterates Object.values(session.trackedBarcodes). It warns not to hold references to session arrays outside the callback.

assertions.text: didUpdateSession is implemented in the BarcodeBatchListener
session.addedTrackedBarcodes is accessed (semantic)
session.updatedTrackedBarcodes is accessed (semantic)
session.removedTrackedBarcodes is accessed (semantic)
session.trackedBarcodes is accessed and iterated (semantic)
A note or comment warns not to hold references to session arrays outside the callback (semantic)
barcodeBatch.addListener( is called with the listener
dataCaptureContext.setMode( is called
<DataCaptureView is rendered in the JSX tree
'-- ENTER YOUR SCANDIT LICENSE KEY HERE --' is present
DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present

evals.prompt: I want to color the barcode highlights differently based on the barcode's symbology: EAN-13 barcodes should be green, Code 128 should be blue, and any other symbology should be hidden (transparent). Use the brushForTrackedBarcode callback. File: IntegratedApp.tsx.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets overlay.listener with a brushForTrackedBarcode callback that returns a green Brush for EAN13UPCA, a blue Brush for Code128, and a transparent Brush (or null) for everything else. A note about the MatrixScan AR add-on requirement is included.

assertions.text: overlay.listener is set with brushForTrackedBarcode implemented
A green Brush is returned for Symbology.EAN13UPCA (semantic)
A blue Brush is returned for Symbology.Code128 (semantic)
A transparent Brush or null is returned for other symbologies to hide them (semantic)
Color.fromHex( or Color.fromRGBA( is used for color construction
new Brush( is present
A note or comment about the MatrixScan AR add-on requirement is present (semantic)
Existing BarcodeBatch mode, DataCaptureView, and camera lifecycle are preserved (semantic)

evals.prompt: Add AR bubble annotations to each tracked barcode in my MatrixScan app. Each bubble should show the barcode's data value and be positioned above the barcode. File: IntegratedApp.tsx.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds a BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay, defines a BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlayView subclass for the bubble UI, registers it with AppRegistry using the moduleName property, sets up the listener with anchorForTrackedBarcode returning Anchor.TopCenter and offsetForTrackedBarcode shifting up, and calls setViewForTrackedBarcode in didUpdateSession. Notes about the AR add-on and New Architecture AppDelegate requirement are included.

assertions.text: new BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay( is present
A class extending BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlayView is defined
AppRegistry.registerComponent( is called with the view's moduleName property (semantic)
overlay.listener is set with anchorForTrackedBarcode returning Anchor.TopCenter or similar anchor (semantic)
offsetForTrackedBarcode is implemented with a PointWithUnit and MeasureUnit (semantic)
setViewForTrackedBarcode( is called with a BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlayView instance
view.addOverlay( is called for the advanced overlay in the DataCaptureView ref callback (semantic)
A note about the MatrixScan AR add-on requirement is present (semantic)
A note about the New Architecture AppDelegate / ScanditReactNativeFactoryContainer requirement is present (semantic)
Existing BarcodeBatch mode, camera lifecycle, and basic overlay are preserved or adapted (semantic)

evals.prompt: How do I pause and resume MatrixScan Batch scanning — for example when the user navigates away and returns? Show me how to disable/enable the mode and the camera. File: IntegratedApp.tsx.
evals.expected_output: The skill shows how to set barcodeBatch.isEnabled = false and camera.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.Off) to pause, and barcodeBatch.isEnabled = true and camera.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.On) to resume. It integrates with AppState or navigation focus events.

assertions.text: barcodeBatchRef.current.isEnabled = false is present for pause (semantic)
barcodeBatchRef.current.isEnabled = true is present for resume (semantic)
switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.Off) is present
switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.On) is present
AppState.addEventListener or useFocusEffect or navigation event is used to detect focus changes (semantic)
dataCaptureContext.removeMode( is called in the cleanup function (semantic)
Existing BarcodeBatch integration is preserved (semantic)

evals.prompt: Set up MatrixScan Batch with the Dot overlay style instead of Frame, and use a single global blue brush for all tracked barcodes (without a per-barcode listener). File: IntegratedApp.tsx.
evals.expected_output: The skill changes the BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay to use BarcodeBatchBasicOverlayStyle.Dot and sets the overlay.brush property to a blue Brush. No brushForTrackedBarcode listener is needed for a global brush.

assertions.text: BarcodeBatchBasicOverlayStyle.Dot is used when constructing the overlay
overlay.brush is assigned a Brush with a blue color (semantic)
Color.fromHex( or Color.fromRGBA( is used
new Brush( is present
brushForTrackedBarcode listener is NOT set (global brush approach, not per-barcode) (semantic)
Existing BarcodeBatch mode, DataCaptureView, and camera lifecycle are preserved (semantic)

evals.prompt: Inside my MatrixScan Batch didUpdateSession, I need to build a list of every currently tracked barcode where each entry has the track's stable id, the decoded value, the symbology, and the corner positions on screen. Show me how to read those fields off each tracked barcode. File: IntegratedApp.tsx.
evals.expected_output: The skill iterates Object.values(session.trackedBarcodes) inside didUpdateSession and, for each TrackedBarcode, reads trackedBarcode.identifier (number), trackedBarcode.barcode.data (string | null), trackedBarcode.barcode.symbology, and trackedBarcode.location (a Quadrilateral with topLeft/topRight/bottomRight/bottomLeft Points). It notes that location is in image-space and requires the MatrixScan AR add-on, and that for on-screen coordinates the quadrilateral must be converted with view.viewQuadrilateralForFrameQuadrilateral. It warns to copy the data out of the callback rather than holding the session reference.

assertions.text: Object.values(session.trackedBarcodes) is iterated inside didUpdateSession
trackedBarcode.identifier is read for the stable track id
trackedBarcode.barcode.data is read for the decoded value
trackedBarcode.barcode.symbology is read
trackedBarcode.location is read for the corner positions
The location is described as a Quadrilateral (topLeft/topRight/bottomRight/bottomLeft) (semantic)
A note that trackedBarcode.location is in image-space and requires the MatrixScan AR add-on is present (semantic)
view.viewQuadrilateralForFrameQuadrilateral is mentioned for converting image-space corners to view coordinates
A warning to copy the tracked data out before the callback returns rather than holding the session reference is present (semantic)

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan Batch app I want the user to be able to tap a highlighted barcode and see its data. How do I detect taps on the basic overlay highlights? File: IntegratedApp.tsx.
evals.expected_output: The skill implements didTapTrackedBarcode on the BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay listener. The callback receives the overlay and the tapped TrackedBarcode, and reads trackedBarcode.barcode.data to react to the tap. A note that the MatrixScan AR add-on is required (and that a barcode whose brush is null cannot be tapped) is included. The existing overlay, mode, and camera lifecycle are preserved.

assertions.text: didTapTrackedBarcode is implemented on the BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay listener
The didTapTrackedBarcode callback receives the overlay and the tapped TrackedBarcode (semantic)
trackedBarcode.barcode.data is read inside the tap callback to react to the tap (semantic)
overlay.listener is the object carrying didTapTrackedBarcode (semantic)
A note that the MatrixScan AR add-on is required for the basic overlay tap callback is present (semantic)
Existing BarcodeBatch mode, DataCaptureView, and camera lifecycle are preserved (semantic)

evals.prompt: I already have AR bubble annotations on my tracked barcodes via BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay. Now I want to handle taps on those AR bubbles so I can open a detail screen. How do I detect a tap on the advanced overlay view for a tracked barcode? File: IntegratedApp.tsx.
evals.expected_output: The skill implements didTapViewForTrackedBarcode on the BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay listener (overlay.listener). The callback receives the overlay and the tapped TrackedBarcode and reads trackedBarcode.barcode.data / trackedBarcode.identifier to route to a detail screen. It notes this is the advanced-overlay equivalent of the basic overlay's didTapTrackedBarcode, that it requires the MatrixScan AR add-on, and that the existing anchorForTrackedBarcode / offsetForTrackedBarcode / setViewForTrackedBarcode wiring is preserved.

assertions.text: didTapViewForTrackedBarcode is implemented on the BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay listener
The didTapViewForTrackedBarcode callback receives the overlay and the tapped TrackedBarcode (semantic)
trackedBarcode.barcode.data or trackedBarcode.identifier is read inside the tap callback (semantic)
overlay.listener is the object carrying didTapViewForTrackedBarcode (semantic)
didTapViewForTrackedBarcode is distinguished from the basic overlay didTapTrackedBarcode (semantic)
A note that the MatrixScan AR add-on is required is present (semantic)
Existing anchorForTrackedBarcode, offsetForTrackedBarcode, and setViewForTrackedBarcode wiring is preserved (semantic)

evals.prompt: I want my MatrixScan Batch app to beep and vibrate the first time each new barcode starts being tracked. There's no automatic beep like single-barcode capture has. File: IntegratedApp.tsx.
evals.expected_output: The skill explains that BarcodeBatch has no automatic feedback (there is no BarcodeBatchFeedback class), so feedback must be emitted manually. It constructs a Feedback (e.g. new Feedback(Vibration.defaultVibration, Sound.defaultSound) or Feedback.defaultFeedback) once, then calls feedback.emit() inside didUpdateSession for each barcode in session.addedTrackedBarcodes, deduping by trackedBarcode.identifier so the same track is not re-emitted on later frames. Feedback, Vibration, and Sound are imported from scandit-react-native-datacapture-core.

assertions.text: A note that BarcodeBatch has no automatic feedback (no BarcodeBatchFeedback) and feedback must be emitted manually is present (semantic)
A Feedback instance is constructed with new Feedback( or Feedback.defaultFeedback
Vibration.defaultVibration is used for the vibration channel (semantic)
Sound.defaultSound is used for the sound channel (semantic)
feedback.emit() is called to emit the feedback
feedback.emit() is called for barcodes in session.addedTrackedBarcodes (the new-this-frame collection) (semantic)
Emission is deduped by trackedBarcode.identifier so the same track is not re-emitted on subsequent frames (semantic)
Feedback, Vibration, and Sound are imported from 'scandit-react-native-datacapture-core'
